Transcrição Completa: How to Recruit Sales People
[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "14 How to Recruit Sales People"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Título com ícones de recrutamento e equipes de vendas
[00:02] Okay, so one of the biggest issues that you're going to have when you're actually scaling up is recruiting more salespeople, setters, closers into the organization. Now, what I do, and we've been doing this for a couple months now on behalf of our clients to help them recruit internal salespeople, is we've been going into a handful of Facebook groups.
[00:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Facebook Groups for Recruitment"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Lista de grupos do Facebook especializados em vendas
[00:25] And we've been posting about job opportunities that we have in a very specific way. I'm going to go through a few of those groups real quick. I want to make sure you understand what these are so you can join them yourself, and so you can post the job opportunities.
[00:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "High Ticket Ninjas Group"
NÚMEROS: 7,000 members
VISUAL: Grupo "High Ticket Ninjas, Closers, Influencers, and Lead Generation"
[00:45] So, first of all, the groups, the first one that I joined into that I've actually had a lot of success in is called High Ticket Ninjas, Closers, Influencers, and Lead Generation. These groups are built literally to post job opportunities.
[01:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Additional Facebook Groups"
NÚMEROS: 6.3K, 3.6K, 12.7K members
VISUAL: Lista de grupos: High Ticket Closers, Sales Jobs, J-Appers
[01:05] Another one, High Ticket Closers-new Opportunities. There's this one called High Ticket Sales Jobs for Inbound Closers and Appointment Setters. That's 3.6K. We have another called High Ticket Closer J-Appers. No paid courses. This one has 12.7K members.
[01:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Example Post: $40K Amazon Automation"
NÚMEROS: $40,000 product, hundreds of weekly qualified leads
VISUAL: Post real no Facebook com copy completo
[01:30] All of these groups, I'm going to give you some examples of what we've done. Here's a recent post for an Amazon automation offer for one of my clients, Elton and Pedro. We've had success pulling, I believe, two or three people up to this point from this specific group.
[01:45] Here's how the copy reads. "Need closers for a $40,000 Amazon automation offer for a client with hundreds of qualified leads coming in weekly." Notice how I put it in bold capitalized. This is what's referred to as a headline.
[02:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Post Structure Breakdown"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Anatomia do post: Headline, descrição, qualificação, offer details
[02:10] I would encourage that you go into the high ticket ninjas group, look at my activity in particular, and just essentially copy that and then fill in the blanks for what your specific offer is. I then describe a little bit about how much the client is currently spending per day, how many leads there are.
[02:30] I make sure that they're qualified. I go over a little bit of what we are asking. In this case, I make sure that they know we're getting people to say yes, they have $40,000 and that they want the Amazon automation offer that are currently uncontacted.
[02:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Qualification Criteria"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Critérios específicos: No agencies, North America only
[02:50] I then add a little bit of qualifying criteria after I describe the offer. You do not want a closing agency, somebody who is going to staff closers into the business. Those people staff absolute trash and they typically staff fat cat closers and not real salespeople.
[03:10] So, we make sure we say this client wants in-house closers only. They are not looking for a closing agency to staff people into the deal. In this case, this particular client has all their leads coming from the United States. So, we also want to make sure that we reference the fact that we need people who are in North America.
[03:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Commission & Call to Action"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Estrutura de comissão e CTA claro para comentar
[03:35] That way, they are on the same time zones. Don't communicate with heavy accents because it does affect the sales process, unfortunately. We then articulate the commission. We also will sometimes communicate any potential bonus incentives. And then we end with a clear call to action.
[03:50] The call to action is to drop a comment below. At which point, we're going to transition to Messenger where we are then going to communicate with people.
[04:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Additional Qualification Filter"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Filtros adicionais contra iniciantes e preguiçosos
[04:05] I then put a few pieces of qualification criteria in addition to what I've already posted. "Do not reach out if you are a noob. We are not looking for beginners. Do not reach out if you are not great in sales. This is not a job for lazy closers who only want leads who watch the video before getting on this call."
[04:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Post Results"
NÚMEROS: 44 interested people in 2 days (Oct 18-20)
VISUAL: Engagement do post com múltiplos comentários
[04:30] Now, as you can see here, this was posted on October 18th in the late afternoon. They have 44 people who had already expressed interest. 43 if you exclude a comment that I had made.
[04:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Profile Screening Process"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Exemplo de verificação de perfil - Meline de San Diego
[04:50] Now, what you're going to get here is a few different comments. What I then typically like to do, like here's Meline as an example. If you hover over her name, we can see that she's in San Diego, California. We can go to her profile and we always want to click on view main profile.
[05:10] We like to see if there's any radical behavior on their profiles or anything that just kind of looks amateur-ish or novice. In this case, Maline's got a relatively private profile. So, to figure out more about Meline, we're going to have to communicate with her via Messenger.
[05:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Profile Analysis: Anthony Moss"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Perfil de Anthony - COO Fempire Media, family man, motivational content
[05:35] Now, moral of the story, back to the post. Here we got Anthony Moss. He says, "Let's go." He currently has himself listed as the COO of Fempire Media. Let's click to his profile to kind of see if there's more about him that we can learn.
[05:50] Looks like a family man. I'll tell you this, we've had a lot of success with guys specifically who have families. Same thing with women. They do extremely well and they're typically very well dedicated.
[06:05] Right away I see on Anthony's profile that it looks like he's posting some type of video that might be motivational from Mike Tyson. Another one from Kobe. That's good right there. It looks like he's married. Motivational content.
[06:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Anthony's Video Example"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Vídeo motivacional de Anthony sobre gratidão
[06:25] And he also looks like he is a man of God. I can also see some videos from this guy. Let's just play it. "Happy Wednesday. Sometimes you just got to get outside early in the morning. Say what you're thankful for. I'm thankful for my family. Thankful for my health."
[06:40] Hey, that seems good. All I'm trying to do here is rapidly qualify. Is this a potential candidate? Anthony seems like a potential candidate.
[06:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Initial Messenger Outreach"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Template de mensagem inicial para prospects qualificados
[06:55] What I'm then going to do is I'm going to reach out to people like Anthony. I'm going to shoot him a message. "Hey, Anthony, appreciate your interest in the opportunity, I would love to know more about you. Can you send me over any flex data or stats that I can share with the client? Tell me about yourself."
[07:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Comment Variety Examples"
NÚMEROS: 800K in sales mentioned
VISUAL: Diferentes tipos de comentários dos interessados
[07:20] Now, wide range of comments. Anthony says, "Let's go." George says, "I'm interested and would like to learn more about the offer." Mike says, "Sold about 800K and done for you already. Interested to hear more and see if it's a good fit."
[07:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Filtering Out Unqualified Candidates"
NÚMEROS: ~50% overseas/unqualified
VISUAL: Exemplo de candidato da Índia ignorando requisitos
[07:40] Now, in addition to this, you will get people who will completely ignore what you have posted. Take Pit here as an example. P, as you can see here, he lives in India. He completely neglected the instructions of the post.
[07:55] So, you will get I'd say about half of the people who are typically overseas who are novice and just not going to be good fits. I typically get anywhere at the low end of like 40 people within the first two to three days and about a fourth of them will be very qualified people.
[08:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Messenger Follow-up: Jason Example"
NÚMEROS: $8,800 product, Excel spreadsheet stats
VISUAL: Conversa no Messenger com candidato qualificado
[08:20] What we then want to do is we want to move over to Messenger. I'll use Jason here as an example. In this case, Jason did a really good job. He articulated himself for how much revenue he generated, what his average closing rate was. He even sent an Excel spreadsheet screenshot of his closing statistics.
[08:40] Could he be lying? Sure. But there's a low probability that he would go and make that up in the speed in which we were messaging with one another. I talked to him about what the price point of the offer was. He said it was an $8,800 product for a company called the salesmentor.
[09:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Multiple Opportunities Strategy"
NÚMEROS: 3 opportunities: funding, info product, Amazon automation
VISUAL: Oferecendo múltiplas opções para o candidato escolher
[09:05] I then looked up that company. In this case, I had three opportunities at the time, a funding offer, an information product offer, and an Amazon automation offer. And I let him pick for which one might be the best solution for him. I then referred him over to that specific client.
[09:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Interview Process"
NÚMEROS: 2 interviews via Zoom
VISUAL: Processo formal de entrevista com role-play
[09:30] What I encourage the clients to do is obviously go through a formal interview process with these people. I typically like to do interviews via Zoom so I can see the person. I also like to do two interviews when we interview salespeople especially. I want to see how they show up twice.
[09:50] I want to go through scenarios with them when I'm on these calls that I'm actually dealing with within my sales organization. I want to ask them questions about objections and how they'd overcome it. I want to do a little role playing with them right there in the interview.
[10:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Fat Cat vs Real Salesperson Test"
NÚMEROS: 100 lead list scenario
VISUAL: Teste prático para identificar tipo de vendedor
[10:15] I want to ask them questions about are you a fat cat closer or are you a real salesperson? Those types of questions would be, hey, I have a lead list of 100 people. What would you do with it to get those people sold? Listen to what they have to say.
[10:30] Maybe they say something crazy like, oh, I wouldn't do anything with those leads. I would expect them to be emailed and put through a booked call funnel. Get them out of there. Versus other people are going to say, I would text them all. I would see if I can re-engage them.
[10:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Good Candidate Responses"
NÚMEROS: None
VISUAL: Respostas ideais sobre follow-up e engajamento
[10:55] I'd probably ask you as the client if there was any videos that I could use to potentially follow up with these people. I'd want to know how nurtured they are. When's the last time we communicated with them? Those types of questions and them expressing things they'd actually do that are beneficial.
[11:10] That's what we care about when we're going through that formal interview process. Go join into all these groups. Start looking at the posts that I've made. Look at all the people that comment. Get them on the interview. Go through the interview process. Role play with them. And then once you're ready to bring them on the team, watch the next video.
🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy DOMINA completamente o recrutamento de vendedores de elite! Enquanto empresas desperdiçam tempo com agências que fornecem lixo, Jeremy vai direto à fonte - grupos do Facebook onde GUERREIROS de vendas se congregam. O processo de screening é BRUTAL e eficiente: elimina fat cats, preguiçosos e amadores em segundos. Jeremy não recruta vendedores - ele CAÇA predadores de vendas que podem DESTRUIR a concorrência!
